title: underscore characters in binary number
body:
Using Iverilog 10.2, this expression is reported as a syntax error:

```verilog
localparam MAC_COMMAND_CONFIG_REG = ‭32'b0000_0100_0000_0000_0010_0000_0010_0000;
```
whereas according to IEEE 1364-2001 sec. 2.5.1:

> The underscore character (_) shall be legal anywhere in a number except as the first character. The underscore character is ignored. This feature can be used to break up long numbers for readability purposes.

Notice that underscores are correctly accepted within hex and dec numbers.
